page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u0.c1m8.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing a total of 8 vCPUs. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates modest compute capabilities. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng and Geekbench place the processor in the bottom 25% and bottom 10% categories respectively, reflecting its older Intel Xeon architecture. Memory read bandwidth is also relatively low, though large-block write bandwidth reaches average performance tiers. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3 operations, show weaker throughput, while LLM inference with small 135M models achieves average speeds but drops significantly for larger 7B models.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and high memory capacity relative to compute power, this instance offers a memory-optimized profile. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require a larger memory footprint but do not demand high-frequency CPU performance. The absence of local storage means it relies entirely on network-attached storage. This configuration is best suited for memory-intensive but compute-light workloads, such as caching layers, small database instances, or lightweight enterprise applications that benefit from 64 GB of RAM without requiring intensive parallel processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u0.c1m8.2xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz and 64.0 GB of RAM. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates modest compute and memory read performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores generally falling into the lower percentiles, while large-block memory write speeds and small-model LLM inference align with average tiers. With a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, compute-light applications. It is best suited for workloads such as caching systems, small database operations, and lightweight enterprise applications requiring a larger memory footprint without intensive processing demands.
